evals.name: Full security posture review
evals.prompt: 이 앱의 보안 상태를 전반적으로 평가해줘
evals.expected_output: Complete Security Posture Report with all 6 domains scored, SPS calculated, and verdict rendered
expectations: Agent follows the 6-step workflow in order (Discovery → Data Classification → Compliance Mapping → Threat Modeling → Domain Scoring → Report)
Output contains all required sections from Output Format template (Application Security Profile, Data Classification, Compliance Matrix, Threat Register, Domain Scores, Verdict, Remediation Roadmap)
SPS is computed using the correct weighted formula
Verdict matches SPS range per the Verdict Rules table
Agent reads actual project files with Read/Grep tools (ALWAYS rule #6)
Every finding in Threat Register has a risk_score = likelihood x impact
Remediation Roadmap is sorted by risk_score descending (ALWAYS rule #5)

evals.name: PIPA compliance check
evals.prompt: 이 프로젝트에서 개인정보보호법 준수 여부를 검토해줘
evals.expected_output: Security Posture Report with PIPA compliance matrix filled with article-level citations
expectations: Compliance Matrix includes PIPA-specific articles (Art. 15, Art. 3, Art. 21, Art. 24, Art. 30, Art. 17)
Each compliance item has article number, not just framework name (ALWAYS rule #2)
Data Classification step is completed before compliance assessment
Status for each requirement is PASS, FAIL, or UNVERIFIED — no other values
UNVERIFIED items state what evidence is needed to confirm (NEVER rule #3)
Agent reads actual code to verify compliance claims (ALWAYS rule #6)

evals.name: Incident response playbook (policy-only)
evals.expected_output: Incident response policy following the Policies section format, without full 6-step audit
expectations: Agent triggers the 'User requests policy only' edge case and skips Steps 1-5
Policy follows the exact Policies section format (Scope, Classification, Requirements, Exceptions, Review cycle, Effective date)
Agent asks for organization name, data types, and team size before drafting (per edge case procedure)
Policy includes numbered, specific, verifiable requirements — not vague guidelines
No SPS score or Domain Scores produced (since full audit was skipped)
